steps.title: Identify the cause-and-effect claim
steps.content: The passage claims that the island's activity directly depends on Peter's presence—it stops when he's away, starts when he returns. A supporting quotation must show this cause-effect relationship explicitly.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D states “Feeling that Peter was on his way back, the Neverland had again woke into life.” This explicitly ties the island's awakening to Peter's impending return.

Choices A, B, and C describe unrelated details—a rock's size, the children's first sight of the island, and Peter's solitude—without showing how the island's activity cycles based on Peter's presence.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
